title: 10 best herbal teas for lymph node swelling
intro_desc: Herbal teas for lymph node swelling are a natural remedy that helps to reduce inflammation and promote the drainage of excess fluids in the body. This can be especially beneficial for individuals who experience swollen lymph nodes due to infections, allergies, or autoimmune disorders. Certain herbal teas, such as Echinacea, Sage, and Peppermint, contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help to soothe and calm the lymphatic system. For example, drinking a warm cup of Echinacea tea can help to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the affected area, improving overall health and quality of life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower teas helps with lymph node swelling because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which are derived from the herb's unique combination of flavonoids and terpenoids. These compounds have been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals in the body, thereby reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with swollen lymph nodes. Additionally, the tea's antimicrobial properties help combat infections that can cause lymph node swelling, promoting overall immune system balance and well-being.
remedy_properties: Inflammation reduction: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in Kansas Coneflower tea, such as echinacoside and caffeic acid, help reduce inflammation in the lymph nodes, alleviating swelling and discomfort.

Immune system support: Kansas Coneflower's immune-stimulating properties, including its ability to increase white blood cell production and enhance natural killer cell activity, help the body fight off underlying infections that may be contributing to lymph node swelling.

Antioxidant activity: The high antioxidant content in Kansas Coneflower tea helps protect against oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to lymph node swelling and inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots have been traditionally used to treat lymphatic congestion and inflammation, making them a valuable part in tea blends for lymph node swelling.
Barks: The barks are rich in compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort associated with lymph node swelling.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2-3 tablespoons of dried echinacea angustifolia roots or 4-6 teaspoons of fresh roots.
Measure and mix the dried roots with boiling water in a teapot for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh strainer or cheesecloth to remove solids.
Add one teaspoon of honey to taste and stir well before serving immediately.
Drink the tea three times a day, 30-60 minutes before meals or as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree teas helps with lymph node swelling because it is rich in antioxidants, which help to reduce inflammation and promote healing. The tea contains compounds like sesquiterpenes and flavonoids that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to alleviate swelling and discomfort associated with lymph node enlargement. Additionally, the tea's antioxidant properties may also help to boost the immune system, allowing it to better fight off underlying infections or illnesses that could be causing lymph node swelling.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Ginkgo biloba's antioxidant compounds, such as flavonoids and terpenoids, help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to lymph node swelling.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Ginkgo biloba extracts have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory effects, which may help alleviate swollen lymph nodes by reducing inflammation and promoting healthy immune function.

Immunomodulatory properties: Maidenhair Tree teas may help regulate the immune response by modulating the activity of immune cells, such as T-cells and macrophages, which can contribute to lymph node swelling.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the maidenhair tree have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing inflammation and swelling associated with lymph node enlargement.
Barks: The barks of the maidenhair tree contain flavonoids and saponins that help to reduce edema and promote blood circulation, which can aid in relieving lymph node swelling.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried ginkgo biloba leaves and measure out two teaspoons for every eight ounces of water.
Boil the water in a pot then reduce heat to simmer while adding the ginkgo biloba leaves.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes or until the tea reaches your desired strength and flavor.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the ginkgo biloba solids.
Allow the tea to cool to a comfortable temperature then drink immediately to reap its benefits.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric teas helps with lymph node swelling because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce inflammation in the body. The bioactive compound curcumin, present in turmeric, has been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines, thereby minimizing lymph node swelling. Additionally, curcumin's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals that can contribute to chronic inflammation, promoting overall health and well-being by reducing lymph node swelling and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Turmeric's active compound, curcumin, has potent anti-inflammatory effects, which can reduce swelling and discomfort associated with lymph node inflammation.

Antioxidant: Turmeric's high antioxidant content helps neutralize free radicals that can contribute to oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, promoting a healthy environment for lymphatic function.

Immune-modulatory: Turmeric has been shown to modulate immune response by reducing excessive inflammation and promoting balanced immune activity, which can help alleviate swollen lymph nodes caused by infections or autoimmune disorders.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in curcuminoids, which have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce lymphatic congestion and swelling.
Rhizomes: Contain a high concentration of volatile oils, which can stimulate the lymphatic system and promote drainage to alleviate swelling.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried turmeric root powder and add it to a heat-resistant cup.
Heat one cup of filtered water in the microwave for 30 seconds to achieve a temperature of 180 degrees fahrenheit.
Pour the heated water over the turmeric powder in the cup and let it steep for five minutes.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into another heat-resistant cup.
Allow the tea to cool for at least 10 minutes before consuming it.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold teas helps with lymph node swelling because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which aid in reducing swelling and alleviating discomfort. The tea's flavonoids and carotenoids also possess antioxidant properties that help combat oxidative stress, a common factor contributing to lymph node inflammation. Additionally, the tea's expectorant qualities help loosen and clear out mucus and other debris from the body, further supporting the body's natural defenses against infection and promoting overall health.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant compounds: Pot marigold tea contains antioxidants such as lutein and zeaxanthin, which help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, thereby alleviating lymph node swelling.

Anti-inflammatory flavonoids: The flavonoids present in pot marigold tea, including kaempferol and quercetin, exhibit potent anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort associated with enlarged lymph nodes.

Immunomodulatory triterpenoids: Pot marigold tea contains triterpenoids like β-amyrin and lupeol, which have immunomodulatory effects, meaning they can modulate the immune response to reduce inflammation and promote healing in swollen lymph nodes.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and saponins, which can help reduce inflammation and improve circulation, aiding in the removal of toxins that may contribute to lymph node swelling.
Flowers: Containing carotenoid pigments, including lutein and zeaxanthin, which have anti-inflammatory properties and can help soothe swollen lymph nodes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried calendula flowers from a reputable source measuring one teaspoon for every cup of water.
Measure two cups of filtered water and bring it to a boil in a medium-sized pot within ten minutes.
Add the dried calendula flowers to the boiling water allowing it to steep for 5-7 minutes releasing the active compounds.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a large cup discarding the solids within five seconds.
Let the tea cool down to a comfortable temperature before consumption serving hot or chilled as desired.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder teas helps with lymph node swelling because of its unique combination of anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory properties. The flavonoids present in elderflowers have been shown to reduce inflammation and improve circulation, which can help to alleviate swollen lymph nodes caused by infections or other conditions. Additionally, the antimicrobial properties of elder teas may also help to combat underlying infections that contribute to lymph node swelling, promoting a return to normal functioning and reducing discomfort.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Elder tea contains antioxidants such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, thereby reducing lymph node swelling.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Elder tea has anti-inflammatory compounds like quercetin and rutin, which can help reduce swelling and discomfort associated with swollen lymph nodes by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals.

Immune system modulation: Elder tea contains immune-modulating compounds that can help stimulate the body's natural defense mechanisms, promoting healthy lymphatic function and reducing inflammation in the lymph nodes.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the elder plant are used due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling in lymph nodes.
Rhyzomes: Elder rhyzomes have been traditionally used to make teas that stimulate the immune system and promote lymphatic drainage, reducing swelling in lymph nodes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest one teaspoon of dried sambucus nigra flowers for every eight ounces of water.
Combine the sambucus nigra flowers with boiling water in a glass teapot or cup.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, allowing the active compounds to infuse into the water.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a large mug or container.
Let the tea cool and consume it within 30 minutes of preparation to maximize its effectiveness.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow teas helps with lymph node swelling because of its unique properties that promote healthy drainage and detoxification. The saponins present in marshmallows create a soothing, protective barrier on the mucous membranes, reducing inflammation and irritation in the lymphatic system. As a result, swollen lymph nodes can begin to drain and clear out excess toxins, allowing for proper functioning and overall immune system support. This natural remedy also helps to reduce puffiness and discomfort associated with enlarged lymph nodes.
remedy_properties: Emollient and demulcent properties: Marshmallow root tea helps to soothe and calm irritated lymph nodes by forming a protective barrier on their surface, reducing inflammation and discomfort.

Anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties: The antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds present in marshmallow tea may help reduce the swelling of lymph nodes by decreasing inflammation and oxidative stress in the surrounding tissue.

Mucilages and saponins: Marshmallow tea contains mucilages (thick, gel-like substances) that can help to absorb excess fluids and toxins from swollen lymph nodes, while its saponin content may also have anti-inflammatory effects on the affected area.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the marshmallow plant contain mucilage, which helps to soothe and calm inflamed lymph nodes, reducing swelling and discomfort.
Barks: The barks of the marshmallow plant have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce lymph node swelling by combating underlying inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried althaea officinalis roots in sufficient quantity for your desired amount of tea.
Combine one teaspoon of dried root material with one cup of boiling water and steep for five minutes.
Strain the tea solution using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove solids and discard.
Allow the tea to cool to room temperature before refrigerating it in an airtight container overnight.
Reheat the cooled tea by combining one cup of the liquid with one cup of boiling water and steeping for an additional three minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion teas helps with lymph node swelling because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The tea contains flavonoids, which have been shown to reduce swelling and alleviate pain in the body. Additionally, dandelion root has natural diuretic properties that help stimulate lymphatic function, allowing for improved removal of toxins and waste from the body. This can lead to a reduction in lymph node swelling, making it a popular natural remedy for those seeking an alternative approach to managing this condition.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Dandelion tea contains flavonoids and polyphenols that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and inflammation in the lymph nodes.

Choleretic action: Dandelion root has choleretic (bile-stimulating) properties, which can improve liver function and enhance the body's natural detoxification processes, including lymphatic drainage and reduction of lymph node swelling.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in dandelion tea, such as vitamin C and beta-carotene, help protect against oxidative stress and cellular damage, which can contribute to lymph node swelling by reducing inflammation and promoting healthy immune function.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in inulin, which helps to stimulate the lymphatic system and reduce swelling.
Fruits: Contain antioxidants and flavonoids that help to reduce inflammation and promote healthy lymphatic function.
remedy_recipe: Gently rinse one teaspoon of dried taraxacum officinale leaves in cold water to remove impurities and debris.
Heat two cups of filtered water in a teapot or kettle for five to seven minutes to reach boiling point.
Add the rinsed taraxacum officinale leaves to the hot water and let it steep for ten to fifteen minutes to infuse the tea with its properties.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a large cup or teapot, discarding the solids and leaving about two cups of liquid.
Allow the tea to cool down slightly before consuming, aiming for a comfortable drinking temperature between 80°f and 90°f for optimal lymph node support.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Goldenseal teas helps with lymph node swelling because it contains berberine, a bioactive compound that has potent anti-inflammatory and immune-boosting properties. Berberine has been shown to inhibit the growth of certain bacteria and viruses, which can contribute to lymph node swelling. Additionally, goldenseal's ability to reduce inflammation and improve circulation may help alleviate congestion in the lymphatic system, allowing for more effective removal of toxins and debris that can cause swelling. This natural remedy may provide relief from discomfort and promote overall wellness.
remedy_properties: Berberine: Goldenseal contains berberine, an alkaloid that has been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with swollen lymph nodes by inhibiting the activity of inflammatory enzymes.

Isoquinoline alkaloids: The isoquinoline alkaloids present in goldenseal tea have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help combat infections that may be contributing to swollen lymph nodes and reduce swelling and discomfort.
Saponins: Goldenseal also contains saponins, which are known for their immunomodulatory effects, helping to regulate the body's immune response and reduce inflammation in swollen lymph nodes.
remedy_parts: Roots: Containing alkaloids like berberine, which has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, helping to reduce swelling and infection in lymph nodes.
Rhyzomes: With a higher concentration of berberine than roots, rhyzomes are used to create teas that target lymph node inflammation and congestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality hydrastis canadensis roots in early spring or fall for maximum potency.
Clean and dry the roots then chop them into small pieces to facilitate steeping.
Combine 1 tablespoon of chopped hydrastis canadensis root with 8 ounces of boiling water and let steep for 10 minutes.
Strain the tea through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a large bowl and discard solids immediately.
Store the cooled tea in an amber glass bottle at room temperature for up to 3 days before consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Slippery elm teas helps with lymph node swelling because it contains mucilage, a natural emollient that soothes and protects irritated tissues. The mucilage in slippery elm tea can help reduce inflammation and congestion in the lymph nodes, allowing for a smoother flow of lymphatic fluid and promoting the removal of toxins and waste products from the body. Additionally, slippery elm's anti-inflammatory properties may also help to reduce swelling and discomfort associated with swollen lymph nodes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory effects: Slippery elm tea contains mucilages that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and discomfort associated with lymph node inflammation.

Demulcent properties: The demulcent nature of slippery elm helps to soothe and protect the mucous membranes, including those in the lymph nodes, by forming a protective barrier that reduces irritation and inflammation.

Antioxidant activity: Slippery elm tea contains antioxidants that help neutralize free radicals, which can contribute to chronic inflammation and lymph node swelling, thereby reducing oxidative stress and promoting overall health.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilage, a thick, protective substance that soothes and calms irritated tissues, reducing inflammation and swelling associated with lymph node swelling.
Barks: Containing a high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce pain and discomfort caused by swollen lymph nodes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ulmus rubra leaves and stems or dried leaf material according to the desired quantity for tea preparation.
Measure one teaspoon of dried leaf material or three teaspoons of fresh leaves into a teapot or infuser.
Heat 8 ounces of boiling water in a kettle or on the stovetop before pouring over the herb mixture allowing steeping for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup discarding solids and composting excess herbs immediately after use.
Consume the warm tea slowly and gently throughout the day taking up to three cups daily for optimal lymph node swelling relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Red clover teas helps with lymph node swelling because it is rich in isoflavones, specifically biochanin A, which has been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory properties. When consumed as a tea, these isoflavones may help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with swollen lymph nodes. Additionally, red clover's ability to promote the removal of excess estrogen from the body can also contribute to its potential benefits in reducing lymph node swelling.
remedy_properties: Flavonoids: Red clover tea contains flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce inflammation and swelling in the lymph nodes.

Phytoestrogens: The phytoestrogens present in red clover tea, particularly isoflavones, may help alleviate symptoms of hormonal imbalances, such as those associated with breast cancer or other hormone-related conditions that can cause lymph node swelling.

Saponins: Saponins are a type of compound found in red clover tea that have been shown to have immune-boosting and anti-inflammatory effects, which may help reduce swelling and improve overall lymphatic function.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins and flavonoids, which help to reduce inflammation and promote lymphatic circulation.
Flowers: High in isoflavones, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties and help to reduce swelling.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried trifolium pratense flowers in the morning after dew has evaporated ensuring optimal potency.
Rinse one teaspoon of dried herbs or two teaspoons of fresh herbs with filtered water gently removing impurities.
Steep the rinsed herbs in boiling water for 5 to 7 minutes allowing flavonoids and saponins to infuse into liquid.
Strain the tea mixture using a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean cup discarding solids instantly.
Allow the tea to cool slightly then consume immediately or refrigerate for up to 24 hours prior to serving chilled.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat lymph node swelling with herbal teas is to consume a combination of Echinacea, Goldenseal, and Ginger. These herbs have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and promote healing. Drink 2-3 cups of tea per day, made by steeping 1 teaspoon of dried herbs in boiling water for 5-7 minutes. This natural remedy can help alleviate symptoms and support the immune system.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to lymph node swelling that are treated with herbal teas are sinusitis, bronchitis, and inflammation. Herbal teas like Echinacea, Goldenseal, and Yerba Santa have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and alleviate congestion in the nasal passages and airways. Other herbs like Ginger, Turmeric, and Slippery Elm soothe sore throats and ease digestive issues associated with these conditions.
